sourceMovie.title: The Lighthouse
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: People revisit The Lighthouse for the same reason it broke through in 2019: clear stakes, memorable faces, and a fantasy rhythm that doesn't stall in the middle. Robert Eggers keeps the ensemble pointed in one direction. The drama and fantasy blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: Tideland
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: Tideland (2005) operates in the same drama as The Lighthouse, but with its own distinct voice and pacing. The tense payoff will feel satisfyingly familiar without retreading the same ground.

recommendations.title: Shutter Island
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: Shutter Island (2010) belongs to the same conversation as The Lighthouse — both anchor their drama in thriller emotion that doesn't let go. The tense undercurrent connects them more than any plot summary would suggest.

recommendations.title: The Witch
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If The Lighthouse's drama stayed with you, The Witch (2016) delivers the same sensation through an entirely different story. The fantasy through-line is the real common thread between them.

recommendations.title: Cold Skin
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: Where The Lighthouse excels in drama, Cold Skin (2017) doubles down on fantasy to comparable effect. The tonal similarities run deep enough that watching both back-to-back feels intentional.

recommendations.title: Breach
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: Breach (2007) is the natural next step after The Lighthouse: same drama DNA, different perspective. The dark streak and thriller elevate it well above a simple genre exercise.

recommendations.title: Cul-de-sac
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: Fans of The Lighthouse who respond to its drama will find Cul-de-sac (1966) immediately familiar. Both films share a thriller sensibility without ever feeling like carbon copies of each other.

recommendations.title: Cast Away
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: There's a reason The Lighthouse fans keep landing on Cast Away (2000) — the drama is unmistakably related even when the plots diverge. Both films operate on the same emotional frequency, especially in their third acts.

recommendations.title: Corpse Bride
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: Corpse Bride draws from the same fantasy well as The Lighthouse, channeling romance in ways that feel unmistakably related. The 2005 film makes a natural companion watch — similar in spirit but distinctly its own.

recommendations.title: War of the Worlds
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: War of the Worlds (2005) captures the thriller appeal of The Lighthouse without simply imitating it. The adventure streak running through both films is what puts War of the Worlds at the top of any "if you liked…" shortlist.

recommendations.title: Beetlejuice
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: The fantasy appeal of The Lighthouse — that funny edge, the comedy — translates cleanly into Beetlejuice (1988). Different setting, same essential satisfaction.

faq.question: What should I watch after The Lighthouse?
faq.answer: Try Tideland, Shutter Island, The Witch—each shares DNA with The Lighthouse while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is The Lighthouse?
faq.answer: The Lighthouse (2019) is best described as Drama, Fantasy, Thriller. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is The Lighthouse worth revisiting?
faq.answer: Viewer scores sit around 7.5/10 on TMDB—high enough that replays are common for people who like this drama lane.
